# Understanding <C72> Through Code

```python

```

When you call <C72> with any argument, it immediately delegates to <B72> and returns whatever <B72> returns. There's no additional processing, caching, or modification of the result. Whether you pass small numbers, large numbers, or any other valid input, <C72> simply acts as a transparent intermediary that forwards the call to <B72> and passes back its return value unchanged.
